sourceText: This implies that individuals naturally invest their capital where it can generate the highest return, which is typically in domestic industries. It emphasizes the efficient allocation of capital within a free market economy.
The rich country's people enjoy a better life than the poor country's people.

This highlights how economic development and increased national wealth lead to an improved quality of life for citizens. It reflects his economic philosophy that increased productivity through free markets and division of labor raises the overall standard of living.
The instability of life amidst political turmoil and war, and the anguish over his inventions potentially being used for destructive purposes.
By immersing himself in intellectual inquiry and scientific discovery, he maintained an unshaken inner peace amidst chaotic reality, creating knowledge that would endure for humanity.
Give me a place to stand, and I shall move the Earth.
This statement demonstrates that with the principle of the lever, even small forces can move enormous objects.
The greatest wisdom is to be able to measure everything.
This emphasizes the importance of the ability to accurately grasp and understand all phenomena and things.
One cannot love what one cannot understand.
This indicates that true love for something requires a deep understanding of it first.

The religious and social backlash faced for proposing a heliocentric model in an era dominated by geocentrism, and the fear of repercussions from his theories. The decades-long wait to complete and publish his research.
Confident in his observations and calculations, he bravely published 'On the Revolutions of the Heavenly Spheres' only as he approached death, thus revealing the truth to the world.
